headline: Lewis Black Backs Ophira Eisenberg's Razor-Sharp Comedy Special
lede: Comedian Ophira Eisenberg is set to torch conventional wisdom with her unapologetic new stand-up hour 'I Used To Be Nicer', landing exclusively on Veeps this month.
body:
Veteran comedian Ophira Eisenberg is ready to detonate expectations with her latest comedy special, bringing her trademark sardonic wit to streaming audiences.

Produced under Lewis Black's Stark Raving Black banner, 'I Used To Be Nicer' promises an unfiltered exploration of modern life's absurdities. The special takes aim at sacred cows like parenting, marriage, aging, and the performative landscape of therapeutic culture.

Eisenberg, known for her work on NPR's 'Ask Me Another' and numerous comedy festivals, leverages her Canadian perspective to deliver incisive social commentary. Her comedic approach blends personal narrative with sharp societal observations, creating a comedic experience that feels both intimate and universal.

Veeps will premiere the special on May 15 at 5 p.m. PT, marking another bold addition to the platform's growing comedy lineup. Black's involvement as producer signals a potential breakthrough moment for Eisenberg's comedic brand.
key_facts: Produced by Lewis Black's Stark Raving Black
Targets topics like parenting and toxic positivity
Eisenberg is an NPR 'Ask Me Another' veteran
